zotero.abstractNote: We present Neural ICP (NICP), a novel approach for registering 3D human scans to a template model. Traditional Iterative Closest Point (ICP) methods are robust but often struggle with large deformations and require good initial alignment. Conversely, recent learning-based methods are fast but often lack the precision of optimization-based approaches. NICP bridges this gap by learning a neural field that predicts the optimal correspondence and deformation for a given scan. Our method is trained on a large-scale dataset of human motions and can be applied to unseen subjects and poses. We demonstrate that NICP achieves state-of-the-art performance on standard benchmarks while being significantly faster than traditional optimization-based methods.
